title: Pan Seared Scottish Lamb Scotch Egg
description: A twist on the classic Scotch egg, this dish features lamb kidney and a double fry method for a crispy exterior, served with a side of umami rich flavors, perfect for a cozy British inspired meal.

ingredients.name: spices
ingredients.notes: paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper

instructions: Prepare the lamb kidney slices by seasoning with spices and letting them sit at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Wrap each lamb kidney slice with a layer of lamb sausage meat, making sure to seal the edges well.
Dip each sausage wrapped kidney slice in the flour, then the egg wash, and finally the cornstarch, pressing the cornstarch gently onto the sausage to adhere.
Heat 1 cup of vegetable oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees and fry the coated kidney slices for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Remove the fried slices from the oil and let them cool on a paper towel lined plate for 5 minutes.
Increase the oil temperature to 375 degrees and fry the slices again for an additional 2 minutes, until crispy and golden brown.
Remove the double fried slices from the oil and place them on a paper towel lined plate to drain excess oil.
Pan sear the slices in a hot skillet with a small amount of oil until browned on all sides.
Serve the pan seared Scottish lamb Scotch eggs hot, garnished with fresh herbs and your choice of dipping sauce.
